caseStudy.title: DriveSoft — Reservation & Ticketing Platform for Srbija Tours
caseStudy.tagline: A production system that runs daily ticket sales and operations for an international bus carrier.
caseStudy.problem: Srbija Tours, an international bus carrier, ran reservations, ticketing and field operations across disconnected, mostly manual tools. There was no online sales channel for passengers, no central passenger database, and field staff had no way to validate tickets on the road. They needed one system to sell tickets online, manage routes and passengers, validate tickets on the spot, handle payments with legally required fiscal receipts, and give both office and field staff a single source of truth — replacing a patchwork of processes that could not scale with growing route and passenger volume.
caseStudy.solution: I built DriveSoft end to end: a Laravel/MySQL backend exposing REST APIs, a React passenger-facing booking site, a React admin panel for operations, and React Native tools for field workers. The backend handles the full transaction lifecycle — seat availability, reservation locking, payment processing, fiscal-receipt generation and ticket issuance — with Redis caching and queued background jobs to keep response times fast under concurrent bookings. The admin panel gives office staff role-based access to manage routes, departures, passengers and daily reporting, while the React Native app lets field workers validate tickets by scanning QR codes on the road.
architecture: Backend: Laravel + MySQL, REST APIs, Redis caching, queued background jobs
Frontends: React passenger booking site + React admin panel
Mobile: React Native tools for field workers (ticket validation on the move)
Integrations: online payments, fiscal-receipt (legal) integration, QR-code ticket validation
features: Role-based admin panel with operational reporting
React Native tools for on-the-road ticket validation
caseStudy.role: Sole full-stack developer. I own the architecture, the Laravel/MySQL backend, both React front-ends and the React Native tools — from database schema and API design through deployment — and I maintain and extend it in production.
caseStudy.result: Online reservations, ticketing and back-office operations now run through one maintained platform instead of scattered manual processes. Passengers book and pay online, office staff manage operations from a unified dashboard, and field workers validate tickets with a phone scan instead of paper lists. Over 150,000 tickets and reservations have been processed through the system, with 100+ internal users relying on it daily across routes and offices.

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Full-stack developer focused on real production systems and business logic.
about.bio: I build and maintain real production systems for booking, ticketing, operations and business automation. For 5+ years I've shipped end-to-end software for the transport industry — Laravel backends (reservations, payments, fiscalization, logistics), React / Next and React Native front-ends, and 10+ live WordPress sites. I'm comfortable taking ownership of an existing codebase, untangling real business logic, and delivering practical solutions that hold up in production — not just clean code in isolation. Currently full-time at Srbija Tours International, with freelance projects on the side. Remote-first, async, Git-based.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about my work, process, and availability.

items.question: Who is Petar Arsić?
items.answer: I'm a full-stack developer based in Belgrade, Serbia, specializing in Laravel backends and React / React Native frontends. For over 5 years I've been building and maintaining production booking, ticketing, and operations systems for the transport industry. My flagship product, DriveSoft, has processed over 150,000 tickets and reservations for international bus carriers.

items.question: What is DriveSoft?
items.answer: DriveSoft is a production reservation, ticketing, and operations platform I built end to end for international bus carriers. It covers online seat reservations, QR-code ticket validation, payment processing with fiscal-receipt integration, passenger and route management, and a role-based admin panel. It's in daily use by 100+ internal users.

items.question: What tech stack do you work with?
items.answer: My core stack is Laravel (PHP) and MySQL on the backend, React and Next.js on the web, and React Native with Expo for mobile apps. I also work with Redis, Docker, DigitalOcean, WordPress, and Webflow. I choose tools based on what the project actually needs, not trends.

items.question: Do you work remotely?
items.answer: Yes. I work remote-first, asynchronous, and Git-based. I'm based in Belgrade, Serbia (CET/CEST timezone), which overlaps well with European and UK business hours. I'm open to full-time, contract, and freelance engagements.

items.question: Can you build a booking or reservation system for my business?
items.answer: Yes — that's my main specialization. I've built complete booking and ticketing platforms from scratch, including online seat selection, checkout, payment processing with fiscal-receipt compliance, QR-code ticket generation and validation, and admin tools for managing operations. You can try a live demo at demo.drivesoft.rs.

items.question: How do you use AI in your development process?
items.answer: I use AI tools to speed up coding, refactoring, debugging, and documentation. Architecture decisions, business logic, code review, and production quality stay under my direct control. AI accelerates the work — it doesn't decide what ships.

seo.title: DriveSoft Case Study — Production Booking & Ticketing Platform
seo.description: How I built DriveSoft end to end: a Laravel/React booking, ticketing, and operations platform processing 150,000+ reservations for international bus carriers.

caseStudyPage.overview: DriveSoft is a full-stack reservation, ticketing, and operations platform I built from scratch for Srbija Tours, an international bus carrier. It replaced disconnected manual tools with a single system covering online booking, ticket validation, payments, and daily operations. The platform has processed over 150,000 tickets and reservations and is used daily by 100+ internal staff across offices and routes.
caseStudyPage.challengeIntro: Srbija Tours, an international bus carrier, ran reservations, ticketing and field operations across disconnected, mostly manual tools.
challengePoints: No online sales channel — passengers had to call the office or buy tickets in person
No central passenger database — records were scattered across spreadsheets and paper logs
Field staff had no way to validate tickets on the road — boarding relied on paper manifests
Payments were processed manually with no automated fiscal-receipt generation
Office, dispatch, and field teams worked from different data sources with no shared view of operations
caseStudyPage.challengeConclusion: They needed one system to sell tickets online, manage routes and passengers, validate tickets on the spot, handle payments with legally required fiscal receipts, and give both office and field staff a single source of truth.
caseStudyPage.solution: I built DriveSoft end to end: a Laravel/MySQL backend exposing REST APIs, a React passenger-facing booking site, a React admin panel for operations, and React Native tools for field workers. The backend handles the full transaction lifecycle — seat availability, reservation locking, payment processing, fiscal-receipt generation and ticket issuance — with Redis caching and queued background jobs to keep response times fast under concurrent bookings.
caseStudyPage.architectureIntro: DriveSoft is structured as a service-oriented monolith: a single Laravel backend serving multiple frontends through REST APIs.

layers.title: Backend
layers.description: Laravel with MySQL. Resource controllers with form request validation, service classes for business logic, and repository pattern for data access. Redis handles session storage, caching of route and departure lookups, and rate limiting. Queued jobs process payment confirmations, fiscal receipt generation, and email notifications in the background.

layers.title: Passenger Frontend
layers.description: A React SPA that guides passengers through route search, departure selection, seat picking, passenger details, and checkout. Connected to the backend via REST API with real-time seat availability.

layers.title: Admin Panel
layers.description: A React admin application with role-based access control. Operators manage routes, departures, passengers, and pricing. Managers access revenue reports, occupancy data, and CSV/Excel exports. Super-admins configure system settings and user permissions.

layers.title: Mobile Tools
layers.description: React Native (Expo) app for field workers — primarily ticket validation via QR code scanning. Works with intermittent connectivity through offline-first data caching and background sync.

layers.title: Infrastructure
layers.description: Dockerized deployment on DigitalOcean. Nginx reverse proxy, automated SSL, and environment-based configuration for staging and production.

bookingFlowSteps.description: Passenger selects origin, destination, and date. The system returns available departures with pricing.
Interactive seat map shows available and taken seats. Selected seats are temporarily locked to prevent double-booking.
Name, contact, and any required travel documents are entered and validated.
Online payment is processed. A fiscal receipt is generated automatically to comply with Serbian tax regulations.
The system generates a ticket with a unique QR code. Confirmation is sent by email.
Field worker scans the QR code with the mobile app. Ticket is validated and the passenger manifest updates in real time.

featuresList.title: Online Seat Reservation
featuresList.description: Passengers search routes, view real-time seat availability on interactive seat maps, and complete reservations through a guided checkout flow.

featuresList.title: QR-Code Ticket Validation
featuresList.description: Each ticket carries a unique QR code. Field workers use the React Native app to scan and validate tickets at boarding, replacing paper manifests.

featuresList.title: Fiscal-Receipt Integration
featuresList.description: Every payment automatically generates a legally compliant fiscal receipt, meeting Serbian tax authority requirements. The integration handles receipt numbering, tax calculation, and regulatory reporting.

featuresList.title: Route & Departure Management
featuresList.description: Admin users define routes with stops, set departure schedules, manage pricing, and track occupancy across departures.

featuresList.title: Role-Based Admin Panel
featuresList.description: Four access levels — operators handle daily bookings, dispatchers manage departures, managers view reports, and super-admins configure the system.

featuresList.title: Operational Reporting
featuresList.description: Daily, weekly, and monthly reports covering revenue, passenger counts, route performance, and occupancy rates. Exportable to CSV and Excel.

caseStudyPage.role: Sole full-stack developer. I own the architecture, the Laravel/MySQL backend, both React frontends, and the React Native mobile tools — from database schema and API design through deployment. I built the initial version, shipped it to production, and continue to maintain and extend it as the business grows. Every layer of the stack — from migration files to UI components to Docker configuration — is my work.
caseStudyPage.impact: Online reservations, ticketing, and back-office operations now run through one maintained platform instead of scattered manual processes. Passengers book and pay online instead of calling the office. Office staff manage operations from a unified dashboard instead of spreadsheets. Field workers validate tickets with a phone scan instead of paper lists. Over 150,000 tickets and reservations have been processed through the system, with 100+ internal users relying on it daily.
caseStudyPage.demoDescription: Open the live demos below — no login required. The passenger demo shows the booking flow, the admin demo shows the operations dashboard.
caseStudyPage.ctaDescription: I build reservation, ticketing, and operations platforms for transport and service businesses.

seo.title: Laravel Backend Development — Petar Arsić
seo.description: Production-grade Laravel APIs, MySQL data models, and business logic. Authentication, role-based access, queued jobs, Redis caching. 5+ years in production, 150,000+ transactions processed.

laravelService.title: Laravel Backend Development
laravelService.subtitle: Production-grade APIs, business logic, and data models — built to handle real transaction loads.
laravelService.what: I build Laravel backends that power production systems — REST APIs with proper authentication, role-based access control, database migrations, queued background jobs, and Redis caching. My backends handle the full transaction lifecycle: data validation, business logic, payment processing, third-party integrations, and operational reporting. Not prototypes — real systems running under real traffic with 150,000+ transactions processed.

process.title: Database-first design
process.description: I start with the data model — migrations, relationships, and indexes — before writing a single controller. Getting the schema right avoids expensive rewrites later.

process.title: API design
process.description: Resource controllers with form request validation, consistent JSON responses, and proper HTTP status codes. Every endpoint is documented and testable.

process.title: Business logic in services
process.description: Complex logic lives in service classes, not controllers. This keeps controllers thin and business rules testable and reusable.

process.title: Production readiness
process.description: Redis caching for hot paths, queued jobs for heavy processing, proper error handling, and environment-based configuration. Ready for staging and production from day one.

laravelService.ctaDescription: I'm available for backend development projects — from building new APIs to extending existing Laravel systems.

seo.title: Booking & Ticketing System Development — Petar Arsić
seo.description: End-to-end booking and ticketing system development. Online reservations, seat selection, payment processing, fiscal receipts, QR-code validation. 150,000+ tickets processed in production.

bookingService.title: Booking & Ticketing Systems
bookingService.subtitle: End-to-end reservation and ticketing platforms — from seat selection to QR-code validation.
bookingService.what: I build complete booking and ticketing systems from scratch — the kind that process real payments, issue legally compliant receipts, and handle daily operations for transport companies. My flagship system, DriveSoft, has processed over 150,000 reservations for international bus carriers. This is my core specialization: I've solved the real-world problems of seat management, concurrent booking conflicts, fiscal compliance, and field ticket validation.

flow.description: Passengers search available routes by origin, destination, and date. The system returns departures with real-time seat availability.
Interactive seat maps show available, reserved, and blocked seats. Selected seats are temporarily locked to prevent double-booking.
Passenger details, payment processing, and automatic fiscal-receipt generation for legal compliance.
The system generates a ticket with a unique QR code. Passengers receive confirmation by email.
Field workers scan QR codes with a mobile app to validate tickets at boarding. Works offline with background sync.
Staff manage departures, view passenger manifests, track revenue, and export reports from a role-based admin panel.

bookingService.ctaDescription: I build reservation and ticketing platforms for transport, hospitality, and service businesses.
